config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Society matters, but making it automatically take priority over individual rights is dangerous because it can be used to justify abuse, censorship, and discrimination. In my view, the state and the community should protect the common good while setting firm limits that defend basic individual rights, especially for minorities and unpopular people. A healthy society is not one that sacrifices the person too easily, but one that balances responsibility with freedom.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami menunjukkan bahwa ketika “demi masyarakat” dijadikan alasan utama, hasilnya sering adalah penindasan terhadap minoritas, pembatasan kebebasan berbicara, dan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan oleh negara. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi hak dasar individu harus menjadi batas keras, bukan sesuatu yang bisa dikorbankan setiap kali mayoritas merasa itu lebih praktis. Masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang haknya dilindungi, bukan dari individu yang terus-menerus diminta mengalah tanpa batas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically outrank individual rights, because that is exactly how abuse gets normalized. From an Indonesian perspective, I understand the appeal of harmony and collective responsibility, but I also know how easily “for the public good” can become an excuse to silence dissent, pressure minorities, or give too much power to authorities. The common good matters, but it should be pursued within clear limits that protect basic rights, especially when the majority is tempted to ignore people who are inconvenient or unpopular. A society is stronger when it can care for the whole without treating the individual as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, pengalaman politik kami menunjukkan bahwa ketika “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai tanpa batas, yang sering terjadi justru perluasan kekuasaan negara dan pengecilan suara minoritas, bukan kebaikan bersama yang nyata. Saya juga tidak setuju dengan gagasan bahwa hak individu itu bisa dianggap sekunder hanya karena mayoritas merasa lebih praktis; justru hak-hak dasar adalah pagar agar masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi alat penindasan. Kepentingan sosial penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa meruntuhkan kebebasan dasar orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically override individual rights. From my Indonesian perspective, I understand the value of harmony and collective responsibility, yet I have also seen how easily “for the public good” can be used to pressure minorities or silence criticism. A society is not truly strong if it treats personal rights as disposable whenever the majority finds that convenient. The better principle is that the common good must be pursued with firm limits that protect basic rights, especially for people who are unpopular or vulnerable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat betapa mudahnya alasan “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk memperluas kuasa negara, membungkam minoritas, dan mengorbankan kebebasan berbicara atau kebebasan beragama. Saya setuju bahwa masyarakat punya kebutuhan nyata, tetapi kebutuhan itu harus dicapai lewat pembatasan yang ketat dan tidak boleh menjadikan hak dasar sebagai sesuatu yang bisa dinegosiasikan setiap kali mayoritas merasa lebih nyaman. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru melindungi individu terlebih dahulu, karena tanpa hak individu yang kuat, “kebaikan bersama” gampang berubah menjadi alat penindasan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
